entry.title: Multiple Selection in ListPicker - Could Someone Help Me?
entry.content:
Dear community,

I am using the ListPicker tool to select multiple texts from a list. How should I configure it so that all the selected texts are saved when I save the data?

What would you recommend?

entry.title: Re: ‌‌Free training + placement assistance for Accountants to transition to SAP FICO Consultants
entry.content: Hi Careernxt team, I have sent couple of emails and haven't received any response. Kindly review and let me know. My name is Richard.

entry.title: Re: IBP Format sheet - Can we do directly in IBP Producti...
entry.content:
No, you do not need to update the IBP Formatting sheet directly in Prod.

You can create & test in Test production, then once it has been confirmed, you can transfer the template in Prod by loading it in Test -> disconnect from Test Planning Area -> connect to Prod -> save template . This is the standard way.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM - Guided Implementation App - How are T...
entry.content: Thank you John. I was able to review the Help Files and they were helpful. However, I still have the premise of my original question. How do the Tasks which are listed make it into the Guided Implementation App? For example, I noticed the total number of Tasks displayed will change depending on which SAP Activate Methodology is assigned. Do they use Tags, Workstreams, Roles, etc. to have the subset of Tasks displayed via the Guided Implementation App?

entry.title: SAC LIVE BW enable Option Default Values "Keep last saved values for dynamic variables" in view mode
entry.content:
Dear all,

when editing a story the option "Keep last saved values for dynamic variables" under Setting is shown for the story as well as for specific charts/tables. 

In view mode the option is not available (although it was there in the past). My question is, how can I enable this in view mode?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: cf login auth failure in CI_CD deployment
entry.content: Yes I had the same issue. It was special characters in password

entry.title: depretitation assets
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We have some assets which correctly calculate the depreciation in the local currency (COP), but we need to make an adjustment in the hard currency (USD)  (tr. ABSO) and for this currency the system does not recalculate the depreciation from the start of the depreciation but from the date, which we must adjust so that the adjustment takes the recalculation value from the initial date?

Best regards,

Yzhak

entry.title: Re: Content Transport Using SAP Cloud Platform Transport Management Service in SAP CPI CF Environme
entry.content: The target destination did not work for me with the deploy service, what worked for me is to create a CAS to represent the target destination, similar to how shown here:Cargando... | Portal de ayuda de SAP

entry.title: deleted
entry.content: deleted

entry.title: Lessons from the Water Resilience Conference: Cape Town, South Africa, November 20th – 21st
entry.content:
Last November 20th and 21st, our Partner Ernst & Young (EY) organized an insights session on innovative strategies and technologies to enhance water resilience and drive digital transformation in this sector. This session was in collaboration with SAP  bringing together leading technology, real-world experience and global expertise in this sector that is relevant for Africa. 

Water resilience across the African continent is critical and water systems must be able to withstand and recover from various challenges such as climate change, population growth and infrastructure challenges. It requires implementing strategies and technologies that ensure a continuous and reliable supply of clean water, even in the face of disruptions.

This includes the use of smart metering platforms, intelligent reporting and satellite imaging to monitor and manage water resources effectively. By building resilience, government/water companies can enhance their capacity to adapt to changing conditions, protect public health and support sustainable development.

Siyabulele Bashe: How a Smart Metering Platform will ensure water resilience in the City of Cape Town

 

We had two intense days of fluent discussions and networking among participants, where everybody learned about the challenges of the water value chain challenges and use cases of relevant digitalization and innovation practices to face current risks and business needs. It was a great exercise in collaboration among peers and partners. Together with strategy-driven keynotes, four major areas drew the attention of the sessions:  cases of water management platforms, customer focus projects, innovations to address current challenges and asset management optimization.

Inspirational keynotes were delivered over the two days of the event. Siyabulele Bashe (pictured above), offered a very insightful presentation on the City of Cape Town strategical approach based on digitalisation to support the City plans to increase customer commitment after the severe draught crisis.  Siham Salie-Abrahams, Partner at EY offered another brilliant keynote entitled Government & Infrastructure topic – Asset Management as a discipline – what does good look like? which raised a very large discussion among all attendees and provided a lot of interesting and useful information (pictured below).

Siham Salie-Abrahams keynote

Not least relevant was the presentation of Inge Opreel, CIO at FARYS (pictured below) about their experience of setting up water management digital platforms and how this is the to-be strategy of many water companies to address many of the current challenges raised by the water scarcity while transforming companies into data-driven organizations.  This presentation become the main focus of the two days sessions by discussing cases and IT approaches for different perspectives.  FARYS CIO update of the Smart Water Platform which is currently being set up together with De Watergroep and Pipa water companies from the Flanders Region became an interesting update on its scalabiity, this is to add new use cases on the existing architecture initially designed for smart metering data handling:  remote data quality monitoring and water balance analytics.  Further discussions lead to use cases around the water loss reduction cases in their (big data) water management platform.

Inge Opreel inspirational presentation

 

Although all discussions and presentations contained elements of innovation in its heart, both from business and technical perspectives, there were discussions and contributions with focal point in local challenges and how the inspirational presentations provided may help to find solutions for their needs.

 

Final part was dedicated to Asset management, which brought interesting perspectives out of the regular discussions on this subject, this is addressing the Capex allocation and operational management for asset and infrastructure construction showcasing the complexity of harmonizing such diverse profiles of project managers, engineers and financial, logistic responsibles, contractors and financial responsibles by leveraging new capabilities and priorities criteria and how proactive maintenance (Asset Performance Management) discipline can help to improve Asset Investment Planning decisions.

Overall, the collaboration among SAP and EY South Africa organizations has raised again the level of expectations of our customers in the  Water and Waste Water industry by providing such high quality inspirational speeches and facilitating fluent and interesting networking to a level that makes very difficult but a motivating challenge to keep the level for next year!

entry.title: Re: Insights Needed: Managing Complex Relationships with...
entry.content:
Hi @ALAE1 

There is very little we can't handle and based on what you describe I would say it's doable but you might have to scope high complexity to cover your implementation cost here.... this is definitely not out of the box and would require an experienced Variable Pay consultant.

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Re: SAP EMS objects
entry.content: Hi Lambert, I was going through the options in the link. But could not able to locate the specific APIs I am looking for. What I am trying to achieve are below: 1) Once out of entitlement model I generate an entitlement (say warranty), to create 2 things a) look for outbound API to create a warranty service contract in S4HANA and b) to simulate how the same warranty can be provisioned to any 3rd party end customer system. 2) Once the warranty support is over, look for outbound APIs to update warranty service order status in S4HANA Thanks Sam

entry.title: Re: Deleting Blog Post
entry.content: Hello @Cornelia -  if it is your own blog post you would like to delete, please send an email to moderators@sap.com and we can help.

entry.title: Re: SAP Retention Process
entry.content: I appreciate the informative blog and the thorough explanation provided. I'm curious about the scenario in which we do not specify a due date for the retention amount. In that case, does the system still maintain the retention amount as it is? Additionally, I would like to know if there is a way for anyone to assist in clarifying or releasing the retention. Thank you for your help!

entry.title: Add multiple "Responsible Person" type properties in the same dimension
entry.content: We have a dimension where we maintain "Accounts/Customers". There are multiple roles that we maintain at the Account level e.g. Client Executive, Delivery Executive, Account Onshore Partner, Account Offshore Partner etc. We would like to maintain these as properties with a "People picker" option available similar to how it is for the Responsible Person column. Is that possible?

entry.title: In-Memory encryption
entry.content:
What are the options for enabling "In-Memory encryption" or "Runtime Encryption" with S/4HANA?

Is this depending on the HW platform on which HANA runs?

Various blogs discusses "Encryption-at-rest" and "Encryption-in-Transit" which is possible with HANA DB's.

Thank you.

RISE with SAP

entry.title: Re: Compensation - Update compensation forms
entry.content:
Hi @Ravi14 

Yes the Bonus Calculation Job can be triggered through this checkbox:

 

 

All the best,

Xavier

entry.title: Re: SAP B1 TB report BP display filtered by GL Accounts
entry.content:
Hi @tamartsulaia0 ,

The only SAP standard report whereby you can use control accounts as parameters is the General Ledger report. It has Opening balance, Debit, Credit and Cumulative balance in local, foreign and system currencies. There is also the possibility of summarizing your report by field(s) according to your criteria.

I hope it helps.

*** If this answer resolves your question, kindly consider marking it as accepted. This helps our community by highlighting useful solutions. Thank you! ***

BR,
Felipe

entry.title: ML Training Status Unavailable
entry.content:
When trying to use ISS product carousels like "Related Products", "Personalized Product Recommendations", "Complementary Products" and "Replenishment Products", we only see that for all of these recommendations the ML Training Status is unavailable.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: ML Training Status Unavailable
entry.content:
Hello, 

As specified in Navigation around Intelligent Selling Services, the user should use Personalized Product Recommendations or Related Products types to enable ML services.

Kind regards, 
Bárbara
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM - Guided Implementation App - How are T...
entry.content: After checking closer, I noticed the Tasks displayed via the Guided Implementation App are assigned the Project Lead Role. I assume this is how they are filtered to show up under Guided Implementation? If so, it makes sense they would be the Tasks assigned to Project Lead.

Email: rjwilson266@gmail.com

Robin

entry.title: Employee as contact person
entry.content:
In the old SAP CRM it was possible to add roles to created employee and make it contact and employee at the same time. 

In SAP Sales Cloud when I open Employee I also do see option called "Is also Contact", but unfortunately it is only visible on Employee in Adaptation. Even though it is marked to be visible "Set as visible" it is not displayed. In the setting there is disclaimer "Select the checkbox to make an element visible on the screen. This feature is available for administrators only. Note that if the element is already set as not visible by SAP, or Cloud Applications Studio, the administrator cannot override the setting." So maybe it is the case.

We do have employees coming from CRM and for them in Employee record I do see ""Is also Contact" since in CRM they have both roles.  The problem is how to add such role (contact) to employee created in SAP Sales Cloud. Is it possible?

entry.title: Re: Remove Discard Draft button in RAP based fiori apps?
entry.content: So far, I haven't found a solution to this. What we did currently is to rename the button instead.

entry.title: Re: Multiple Selection in ListPicker - Could Someone Help...
entry.content:
It will depend on your use case.  When you say you want to save all selected texts from the multiple selection list picker what does that mean?  Are you wanting to concatenate all the values into a string?  Are you wanting to create one record per selected text?  What does your service expect for how the data is structured when saving?

Can you please elaborate on your scenario?  Thanks

entry.title: How to find ACTVT Activity field / value in program?
entry.content:
Hello,

I have to set up an authorization object in a program. The user wants choose which roles can perform which actions, so I'm guessing the ACTVT needs to be added to the authorization object. 
However, I can't find the variable for the ACTVT in the program. Does anyone know what field this is, or how to retrieve it?

Many thanks in advance!

Romy

entry.title: Denmark Localisation for SAP Business One
entry.content:
Hi All,

I hope you are well.

I would appreciate some clarification on the status of the Denmark Localisation for SAP Business One.

In the link attached below, you can find a list of registered bookkeeping systems that have been checked and registered by the Danish Business Authority. 

Directory of registered bookkeeping systems | The Business Guide

Apparently SAP Business One does not meets the new rules for digital bookkeeping.

Does anyone know what is the current situation?

Thank you,

Kinds regards,

Miguel Angel

Hi Umasaral,

Thanks for your answer , In the above help document for monitoring email with Email transmission app, we are getting emails that are triggered from s4 environment, but the emails that are triggered from SAP BTP CAPM application is not shown, Is there any specific config we need to do for this to show mail status in the app or Email monitoring app is to show email status from s4 environment.  Kindly help me through

entry.title: Issues for generating OData multipart batch/changeset with CL_HTTP_CLIENT
entry.content:
I had some weird issues with CL_HTTP_CLIENT 7.40 SP23 for generating an HTTP request to consume an OData V2.0 service, e.g. a simple creation (POST), and to help other people, I'm posting a solution right now for generating this kind of HTTP request with 2 nested multipart and an application/http message at the lowest level:

  • POST .../$batch with Content-Type: multipart/mixed; boundary=batch_xxxxxx
    • Content-Type: multipart/mixed; boundary=changeset_xxxxxx
      • Content-Type: application/http (the body is the POST request)

The weird issues were for instance:

  • a multipart/form-data content-type without boundary definition,
  • a loss of the contents inside the changeset,
  • a content-type duplicated from the inner multipart,
  • etc.

In fact, all these issues come from a wrong usage of the CL_HTTP_* methods, methods called in the wrong order, etc.

NB: it's just for test purpose, to be used in an old ABAP system, I don't want to log in a recent ABAP system to generate a Service Consumption Class (How to use the OData Client Proxy in SAP S/4 HANA ... - SAP Community).

The final HTTP request should look like this:

POST https://dummy/sap/opu/odata/sap/MD_CUSTOMER_MASTER_SRV_01/$batch?sap-client=100 HTTP/1.0
Content-Type: multipart/mixed; boundary=847ED772BCB1300A1800E26E1B3AE6F50
Content-Length:          481
x-csrf-token: x2VarmW8AM0BAsY_flyWlg==
dataserviceversion: 2.0

--847ED772BCB1300A1800E26E1B3AE6F50
Content-Type: multipart/mixed; boundary=847ED772BCB1300A1800E26E1B3AE6F51

--847ED772BCB1300A1800E26E1B3AE6F51
Content-Type: application/http

POST C_BusinessPartnerCustomerCreate?sap-client=100&BusinessPartner=''&BusinessPartnerCategory='1'&BusinessPartnerGrouping='' HTTP/1.0
Content-Type: application/json
Content-Length: 0
dataserviceversion: 2.0


--847ED772BCB1300A1800E26E1B3AE6F51--

--847ED772BCB1300A1800E26E1B3AE6F50--

Sandra

entry.title: Re: Issues for generating OData multipart batch/changeset...
entry.content:
You may use this ABAP code to generate the HTTP request shown above. It's shown in the variable final_xstring_request in UTF-8 encoding:

DATA(host) = `https://dummy`.
cl_http_client=>create_by_url( EXPORTING url    = host
                               IMPORTING client = DATA(client) ).
client->request->set_header_field(
    name  = '~request_uri'
    value = |{ host }/sap/opu/odata/sap/MD_CUSTOMER_MASTER_SRV_01/$batch?sap-client={ sap_client }| ).
client->request->set_method( 'POST' ).
client->request->set_header_field( name  = 'x-csrf-token'
                                   value = 'x2VarmW8AM0BAsY_flyWlg==' ).
client->request->set_header_field( name  = 'DataServiceVersion'
                                   value = '2.0' ).
client->request->set_content_type( 'multipart/mixed' ).

DATA(multipart_batch) = client->request->add_multipart( suppress_content_length = abap_true ).
multipart_batch->set_content_type( 'multipart/mixed' ).

DATA(multipart_changeset) = multipart_batch->add_multipart( suppress_content_length = abap_true ).
multipart_changeset->set_content_type( 'application/http' ).
cl_http_client=>create_by_url( EXPORTING url    = host
                               IMPORTING client = DATA(client_bp_cust_create) ).
client_bp_cust_create->request->set_method( 'POST' ).
client_bp_cust_create->request->set_header_field( name  = 'Content-Type'
                                                  value = 'application/json' ).
client_bp_cust_create->request->set_header_field( name  = 'DataServiceVersion'
                                                  value = '2.0' ).
client_bp_cust_create->request->set_header_field(
    name  = '~request_uri'
    value = |C_BusinessPartnerCustomerCreate?sap-client={ sap_client }&BusinessPartner=''&BusinessPartnerCategory='1'&BusinessPartnerGrouping=''| ).
DATA(xstring_request_bp_cust_create) = client_bp_cust_create->request->to_xstring( ).
multipart_changeset->set_data( xstring_request_bp_cust_create ).

DATA(final_xstring_request) = client->request->to_xstring( ).

entry.title: Re: Using Logstash with Hybris
entry.content: how we can have more detail about it ?

entry.title: Re: SSO to be enabled while launching BW/4HANA cockpit fr...
entry.content:
Hi

 

Check the FAQ note:

2918492 FAQ: BW/4HANA 2.0 Cockpit Setup

 

===================================================

  1. Configure single-sign-on so the launchpad can be started without entering user/password. This is especially needed if you open BW/4 Cockpit functionality from the BW modeling tools, because without single-sign-on each navigation from the modeling tools to the BW/4 Cockpit will show a logon screen. The logon credentials used in the modelling tools are not forwarded to the BW/4 Cockpit.
    • There are many options to implement SSO, it depends on the your business processes and IT setup. Best place to start is the documentation
    • You might wonder why transaction BW4WEB is able to launch a WebUI without a logon popup. SAPGUI supports a special short-lived one-time token that is used for this navigation from SAPGUI to the browser, but this mechanism does not work when navigating from the BW Modeling Tools. Background information about this technique can be found in note 612670.

===================================================

 

br

Roland

entry.title: Re: SAP BW 7.5 migration to new box
entry.content:
Transporting BW objects (metadata) from lower release to higher releases in general should work until the source and target release is within the same main release. See the consulting note:

2149282 - Cross-release transports of BW metadata

So a direct transport from 7.00 to 7.50 would be a problem.

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio Process Intelligence will not accept the...
entry.content:
Hello Thomas, 

thanks for your question. 

The error you're encountering indicates that SAP Signavio Process Intelligence doesn't fully support standard SQL syntax in the SQL Filter section. The filter configuration often expects just a conditional expression, not a full SQL query.

Try this instead:

In the SQL Filter section, use only the condition itself, like this:

closed_at IS NOT NULL

Why this works:

  • The filter expects a condition rather than an entire SELECT statement.
  • SAP Signavio internally handles the data retrieval, so you only need to specify what rows to filter.

Let me know if this resolves the issue! Otherwise please open a ticket in SAP for Me so that our SAP Signavio Support Team can take a look. 

Many thanks, 
Cäcilia

entry.title: Re: change log for a specific user in HANA
entry.content:
hi,

We have a similar issue, were you able to find the solution.

Please share your knowledge on this .

 

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Importing Diagrams from Solution Manager to Signavio...
entry.content:
Dear Daniel, 

thank you for your question. These issues with inconsistent diagram synchronization could occur due to various reasons. Please open a ticket in SAP for Me (Login with S-User ID and password) so that our SAP Signavio Support Team can investigate on it. 

In general potential causes could be: 

  1. Missing or misconfigured mappings between Solution Manager's structures and Signavio's format can lead to incomplete or inaccurate transfers.
  2. Versioning conflicts: differences between diagram versions in Solution Manager and the imported version in Signavio may cause elements to be missing.
  3. If diagrams are too large or complex, the connector might time out or skip elements during the transfer.

Kind regards, 
Cäcilia

entry.title: RAR Direct Posting - UI for Postponed ReconKeys
entry.content:
Hello,

In case of Direct Posting, how do business users identify the contracts with Postponed Reconciliation keys? The app Display Shifted and Suspended Revenue history does not retrieve them. The only way I found is to query directly the FARR_D_RECON_KEY table filtering with Status R. But it is not for business users.

Thank you,

Christine

entry.title: Re: Need some clarification or suggestions regarding T-Co...

Regards,

Aravind

entry.title: Re: CER006-Cloud Certification
entry.content:
Hi @Krishna65 
You can purchase  one or six SAP Certification exam attempts.
To stay certified, you have a simple path with a subscription to SAP Learning Hub, with access to short and easy yearly assessments.

Alternatively, if you don’t have an SAP Learning Hub subscription, you still have the option to purchase and retake an SAP Certification exam every year.

Please also check Preparing for an exam - FAQ where you find helpful guidance

Please let me know in case you need any further support.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Signavio Scenario Modelling
entry.content:
Hello TC3, 

many thanks for your question. There exist various options within SAP Signavio to display hierarchies. Please find some ideas below: 

  1. Leverage Value Chain Diagrams/Navigation Maps:
    • Value Chain Diagrams are ideal for representing high-level process flows (Level 1 or 2).
    • You can link each high-level process to more detailed BPMN diagrams or Process Models.
    • This allows users to click through to drill down into lower levels (like in Solution Value Flow).
    • E-Learning: Navigation Maps and Value Chains ; Documentation: Process Hierarchy

  2. Use Folders for structuring levels: 
    • Organize your processes into nested folders representing different process levels (Level 1 to Level 7).
    • Each folder should contain models corresponding to that particular level.

  3. Create navigable links between diagrams: 
    • Sub-processes within a BPMN model can be linked to more detailed models (like Level 4 or Level 5).
    • When viewing the diagram in the Collaboration Hub, users can click on sub-process icons to navigate deeper.

Hope that helps. In case of further questions feel free to reach out to your SAP Signavio contact. 

Warm regards, 
Cäcilia

entry.title: Re: Configuring Embedded SAC in SAP Sales and SAP Service Cloud Version 2
entry.content:

Best,
Devin

entry.title: Cannot set Returns flag in Purchase order/return flag greyed out
entry.content:
Good afternoon all,

I am trying to run the returns process.

I have booked a 122 movement on the original material document, which move the stock from Blocked stock to On-order stock and a purchase order was automatically created.

However, when I enter the purchase order, I see that the Returns flag is not set and that I cannot set it manually, because the checkbox is greyed out.

What is causing this and how can I solve it?

Kind regards,

Floyd

entry.title: Re: Synch local dimension members between models
entry.content:
Hi, I could now find the following solution to synchronize local dimensions between planning models.

  1. Create a API Subscription in the source model
  2. Create an OAuth Client
  3. Create an OData Connection and connect to OAuth Client (connection to itself)
  4. Create data model import from OData Connection and import master data of the local dimension

This technique works perfect and offers additional opportunities to replicate (master) data even between different SAC systems.

Will write a blog when I´ve some time and link it here.

Regards,

Thomas

entry.title: Re: Multiple Selection in ListPicker - Could Someone Help...
entry.content: I have certain data in a ListPicker, for example: {1 A, 2 B, 3 C ,4 D, 5 F, 6 G,} I select (A, B, and F,) and then click the save button. I get the save message, but when I check the details page, it only shows the A value. I also check the database, and it only saves the A value. However, I need to see and save the selected values (A, B, and F). I’ve tried several configurations, but it keeps saving only one value. I tried setting up a rule, but I’m not sure where to place it. What else should I do?

entry.title: Unable to fetch values from expand entity in MDK - SAP Asset Manager 2305 Application.
entry.content:
Hi MDK Experts,

We have defined and created a navigation for the MyWorkOrderHeaders entity set i.e.,ZXXX_Nav.
When we try to run the query in the gateway client also it works but when I try to query it by using the expand property or / it does not show any value, or it shows undefined.

const query1 = `$expand=ZXXX_Nav&$filter=ZXXX_Nav/ZOrderId eq '${context.binding.OrderId}'&$top=1`;
const query2 = `$expand=ZXXX_Nav&$filter=OrderId eq '${context.binding.OrderId}'`;

When I try to read the service and pass the query and put the result in the alert box the ZXXX_Nav is empty and does not show any fields/properties.

Can you please help me out here?

entry.title: Re: Story Report - Get employee's age in years and months
entry.content: We should raise a customer influence to get the required result. Same requirement we also have. However, we are able to cater only half of it. The age is getting calculated accurately. But it cannot display in Years and Months format. It will give in decimal only.

entry.title: SRT_MONI error /scmtms/tend Message number 303
entry.content:
Implementing broadcast tendering when the carrier responds with a submitted net price amount via EDI we are receiving the following errors in SRT_moni.

Cannot create freight quotation for freight RFQ 00000000000612869996

------id 303(/SCMTMS/TEND)

entry.title: How do I find the system inventory on hand during a cycle count? Which tables do I look in?
entry.content:
I am creating a report outside of SAP in a data warehouse that contains SAP tables. I am trying to build a view that shows the result of cycle counts and how much they are off by the inventory in the system, how often the counts are off, etc.

I think I have found the cycle count data in the IKPF and ISEG tables, field ISEG.MENGE has the difference in quantity from the count. What I am looking for is what the system inventory number was at the time of the count. Which tables should I use for that where I can tie it back to the time of that Cycle Count?

Would LINK and LINP tables hold these system inventory numbers? And if so, how do I join them to IKPF and ISEG? 

Or, should I be using MSEG? I see that there is a direct link between ISEG and MSEG, but I don't know which fields in MSEG would hold the System Inventory numbers at the time of count.

Any thoughts?

entry.title: Re: Multiple Selection in ListPicker - Could Someone Help...
entry.content: In answer to the last question, I am using the key-value tool to view the data. i only need to view and save, it does not have a defined structure. How can I achieve this?

entry.title: Create a SQL user with access to multiple spaces in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Suppose you want to access two SAP Datasphere spaces using the same SQL user, how do you create such a user? That’s what we will go through in this blog post.

As an example, we have two spaces as the below screenshot shows. We will create a SQL user that can access both of these spaces. For this we take the following steps.

  1. Create a Database User Group including an admin user;
  2. Create an SQL user with the new admin user; 
  3. Create a Database User for each space;
  4. Grant read access to the views that you want the SQL user to have access to.

Figure 1: Two spaces for which we will create one SQL user with read access to both spaces

Step 1 - Create a Database User Group including an admin user

In the side navigation area of SAP Datasphere, choose System > Configuration >  Database Access >  Database User Groups. Here, create a Database User Group. This will provide you right away with administrator credentials. If you need documentation on the creation process, go to this help page. In there, there is also a link to the SAP HANA Cloud documentation that describes the Database User Group Concept.

Figure 2: Dialog for creating a Database User Group

Step 2: Create an SQL user with the new admin user

After the Database User Group is created, go to your preferred database client tool to log on with that user. In this blog post we use the SAP Database Explorer. With the admin user, we create a new SQL user. This will be the user getting read access to the two SAP Datasphere spaces.

 

CREATE USER DWCDBGROUP#READ_SPACES#BOBBY PASSWORD "<A1a etc.>" SET USERGROUP DWCDBGROUP#READ_SPACES

 

Now log on with the newly created SQL user. Tip: in DB Explorer, you can right click on the existing instance and choose “Add Database with Different User” - then it adds a new user while re-using the instance details, which saves you some time.

Figure 3: Add new Database Instance entry with the newly created SQL user

When we check the views in the two spaces that this user has access to, we see that currently no views are accessible.

Figure 4: The newly created SQL user has no privileges yet on either of the two spaces

Step 3: Create a Database User for each space

To grant read access from the two spaces to this SQL user, you first have to create a Database User in each of the spaces. You do this under Space Management > Database Access > Database Users for each of the spaces. Make sure you tick the checkboxes for Enable Read Access (SQL) and With Grant Option. Deploy the space to activate the user and fetch the credentials. 

Figure 5: Dialog to create a Database User for a given space

Step 4: Grant read access to the views that you want the SQL user to have access to

Using the database users created in step 3 - one for each space - we go through the following for each of the spaces.

Check to which views the database user has access. The following statement gives you a list of the views that are exposed for consumption. If you want to add more views, you'll have to expose them for consumption in the SAP Datasphere view editor.

 

select OBJECT_NAME from effective_privileges
where SCHEMA_NAME IN ('SEFAN_SALES','SEFAN_HIERARCH2') AND GRANTEE_TYPE = 'USER' AND IS_GRANTABLE = 'TRUE' AND user_name = current_user;

 

As you can see, this gives us a the list of views exposed for consumption in our space.

Figure 6: List of the views that the space database user has access to

The space database user also has grant select privileges, because we made that setting when creating the user. Therefore, we can fire GRANT SELECT statements and provide our SQL user with read access to the views. You cannot grant access on the entire schema, only on individual objects. SAP Datasphere does not provide you with any user that has full schema access with a grant option.  Below we grant the SQL user access to one view.

 

GRANT SELECT ON SEFAN_SALES."vBusinessPartner" TO DWCDBGROUP#READ_SPACES#BOBBY

 

When now checking again the list of views with the SQL user, you can see that this view popped up. Below screenshot is made after we repeated all of the above step also for our other space.

Figure 7: Views in the SAP Datasphere space that the SQL user has read access to

Build a procedure to grant access to all consumable views of a space at once

Granting read access to all individual views in a space can be a bit cumbersome, but of course you can automate this by coding a procedure that loops over all consumable views for a space and then grants read access to each view. Below code creates such procedure. Make sure to create the procedure with the Database User Group Admin, and then grant access to that procedure to the database users of the space. These can then execute this procedure.

 

--execute with ADM user
CREATE OR REPLACE PROCEDURE grant_select_on_objects (
    IN IP_SPACE NVARCHAR(256),
    IN IP_SQL_USER NVARCHAR(256)
)
LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT
SQL SECURITY INVOKER
AS
BEGIN
    -- Declare a cursor for the SELECT statement
    DECLARE CURSOR C FOR 
        SELECT SCHEMA_NAME, OBJECT_NAME 
        FROM effective_privileges 
        WHERE SCHEMA_NAME = :IP_SPACE
          AND GRANTEE_TYPE = 'USER'
          AND IS_GRANTABLE = 'TRUE'
          AND USER_NAME = CURRENT_USER;

    -- Variables to hold the fetched data
    DECLARE v_schema_name NVARCHAR(256);
    DECLARE v_object_name NVARCHAR(256);
    DECLARE v_sql NVARCHAR(5000);

    -- Loop through the result set
    FOR cur_row AS C DO
        v_schema_name = cur_row.SCHEMA_NAME;
        v_object_name = cur_row.OBJECT_NAME;

        -- Construct and execute the GRANT statement
        v_sql = 'GRANT SELECT ON "' || v_schema_name || '"."' || v_object_name || '" TO "' || :IP_SQL_USER || '"';
        EXECUTE IMMEDIATE :v_sql;
            
    END FOR;

END;

GRANT EXECUTE ON grant_select_on_objects TO SEFAN_SALES#READ_SPACES;
GRANT EXECUTE ON grant_select_on_objects TO SEFAN_HIERARCH2#READ_SPACES

 

When the procedure is created, execute it with the space database users as follows:

 

CALL DWCDBGROUP#READ_SPACES#ADM.grant_select_on_objects('SEFAN_HIERARCH2', 'DWCDBGROUP#READ_SPACES#BOBBY');
CALL DWCDBGROUP#READ_SPACES#ADM.grant_select_on_objects('SEFAN_SALES', 'DWCDBGROUP#READ_SPACES#BOBBY');

 

As you can see below, the SQL user now has access to the views from both spaces.

Figure 8: Views in the SAP Datasphere space that the SQL user has read access to

Because the access is for individual objects, when you want to add views that have been newly created or exposed since, you'll have to run the procedure or the individual statements again. 

Conclusion

Now you have a SQL user with read access to two spaces. Are you happy now?

entry.title: Item category CBXL
entry.content:
How do I assign the item category CBXL to the Sales document type OR/Item category group CBOR?

I need to be able to create orders with bought-in items that are FOC, but without adding a price and without needing to create an invoice.

Kind Regards,

Cecilia

entry.title: Re: Multiple Selection in ListPicker - Could Someone Help...
entry.content: Your Save button will most likely be calling CreateEntity or UpdateEntity and setting the properties like in your screen shot. If you only have one property and are intending to store multiple values in that one property you need to decide how you will structure the data you are saving. If your Odata service has a parent / child structure where your multiple values are stored as multiple child records for the parent then you need to have a rule to loop over the multiple return values and call create/update/delete the child records based on the selection. It all depends on your Odata service structure and what you are trying to achieve when saving the data.

entry.title: Is there a option in Numeric Point chart to display the shown number in words?
entry.content:
The requirement is to display the number (average, grand total% or revenue etc), shown in the numeric point chart to be shown in words as well.

For ex1: If the average of sales = 1,228,876. then below this value, it should display as "1 million, two hundred twenty eight thousand and eight hundred seventy six".

For ex2: If the average Tenure of an employee in the organization is 2.80, then the numeric point chart should be able to show the same value in words as " Two years and 8 months".

entry.title: I have observed an issue in CAP based Application. Can CAP team fix it ?
entry.content:
Launch sample application mentioned in capire documentation. http://localhost:4004/ Home page. CLick on Browse Books -> Preview ‒ List of AdminService.Books dropdown : Click on All my Apps : Again click on Preview ‒ List of AdminService.Books down Arrow. UI is distorted. Please refer screenshot Attched. 

Same thing happens in Actual Application. Exmple : Login to SCT Home page : Click on Manage File Upload tile :Manage File Uploads dropdown : All my Apps : Again click on Manage File Uploads dropdown down Arrow. Page is distorted.  Refer SCT screenshots Attched.

entry.title: Can we get the data in table format of the chart in story reports without creating a table
entry.content:
requirement is when user clicks on the chart in story, the tabular format of the chosen chart should get downloaded. no additional work or any cross tabular table should be created should be done by users.  

Regards,
Nagavalli B N

entry.title: Re: How to display batches with stock only?
entry.content:
Hi,

For a detailed step-by-step on how to configure the batch determination, please take a look at the following Blog post:

  • Batch Determination Setup Scenario in S/4HANA Cloud

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: How to Consume a class as and API or Consume it as an...
entry.content:
Hi Sunil, 

Is the short dump getting triggered at the io_response->set_data( lt_output ) statement? If so, I think it is because the data type of the internal table lt_output does not match the CDS custom entity type. You can try defining lt_output as shown below:

DATA lt_output TYPE TABLE OF ZStockAgeEntity.

I am not sure if I fully understood all your questions, but here are some relevant points that might help:

1. CDS Custom Entity Limitations:

A CDS custom entity cannot be used as a data source in other CDS entities or in ABAP SQL. 

https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_cp_index_htm/CLOUD/en-US/abencds_f1_define_custom_entity.html

2. Filter and Search Capabilities:

It is possible to implement filter and search operations within a CDS custom entity.

https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-rap/implementing-unmanaged-query

Hope this helps.

Best regards,

Krishnan

entry.title: Re: Unable to fetch values from expand entity in MDK - SA...
entry.content:
Since you mention Asset Manager, most likely the entity set the navigation property refers to is not part of the offline store so the data isn't available offline.  Please ensure your offline initialization (and download depending on how you have it configured) includes the entity set referenced by the navigation property.  You may need to run your download or reset the client to reinitialize the offline store.

You can also use ilodata with the UDB files to verify if the data is available offline as expected.

entry.title: Re: Error in SMQ2 "Item 000010: No sales quantity is save...
entry.content:
Hi 

We have a thread and blog for the same. see if you have not gone through that.

https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-q-a/item-000010-no-sales-quantity-is-saved-for-item/qaq-p/471493

S/4 HANA EWM Two Steps Cross Company Process and E... - SAP Community

It seems good to go ahead if you have not gone through that

 

Regards

Rashid Khan

entry.title: Re: 4E9-New Project Billing Configuration and Process

entry.title: SAP TDF – Melhoria de Performance nas views de Imposto Retido e Participante
entry.content:
Olá, pessoal 

Recentemente, entregamos melhorias de performance nas views de CTR PARTICIPANTE e NF_IMPOSTO_RETIDO nas notas 3462516 e 3520967 SP18 e a nota 3538136 para o SP19. A solução TDF já está atualizada para consumir a melhoria entregue nas notas, porém implementações terceiras precisam ser ajustadas para poderem usufruir das mudanças de performance. Nesse blog post vamos descrever os novos parâmetros criados e prover dicas de como as melhorias das views mencionadas anteriormente podem ser consumidas. 

Otimização da view PARTICIPANTE 

Uma parte da informação dessa view vem da Nota Fiscal, por isso, criamos os seguintes parâmetros que podem ser usados para diminuir o volume de notas fiscais selecionadas: 

  • P_DT_INI: Data inicial da nota fiscal, comparado com o campo Document date (DOCDAT). 
  • P_DT_FIN: Data final da nota fiscal, comparado com o campo Document date (DOCDAT). 
  • P_DT_E_S_INI: Data inicial da nota fiscal, comparado com o campo TMF Reporting Date (ITMF_REPDAT). 
  • P_DT_E_S_FIN: Data inicial da nota fiscal, comparado com o campo TMF Reporting Date (ITMF_REPDAT). 
  • P_DO_NOT_USE_ONE_TIME_PARTNER: Caso seja informado ‘X’, participantes derivados na nota fiscal não serão considerados na consulta. 

Como a informação oriunda do ERP SAP não possui campo de data, o número de registros só tende a crescer. Recomendamos o uso dos seguintes campos da PARTICIPANTE como filtros: 

  • Participant Code (COD_PART) 
  • Partner Type (Vendor or Customer) (TIPO_PART) 

O uso desses filtros irá melhorar de uma forma significativa suas consultas que envolvem a view PARTICIPANTE. 

Otimização da view NF_IMPOSTO_RETIDO 

A view NF_IMPOSTO_RETIDO é composta por informações oriundas da PARTICIPANTE, impostos retidos do FI e de notas fiscais. Os seguintes parâmetros podem ser providos: 

  • P_MANDT: Filtro de client, relevante para todos os casos. 
  • P_EMPRESA: Empresa, relevante para filtrar tanto dados vindo de nota fiscal, quanto dos impostos retidos do FI. 
  • P_DT_INI: Data inicial da nota fiscal, comparado com o campo Document date (DOCDAT). 
  • P_DT_FIN: Data final da nota fiscal, comparado com o campo Document date (DOCDAT). 
  • P_DT_E_S_INI: Data inicial da nota fiscal, comparado com o campo TMF Reporting Date (ITMF_REPDAT). 
  • P_DT_E_S_FIN: Data inicial da nota fiscal, comparado com o campo TMF Reporting Date (ITMF_REPDAT). 
  • P_DO_NOT_USE_ONE_TIME_PARTNER: Caso seja informado ‘X’, participantes derivados na nota fiscal não serão considerados na consulta. 

Adicionalmente, recomendamos as seguintes boas práticas durante o acesso da view: 

  • Utilizar os filtros de PARTICIPANTE mencionados anteriormente: COD_PART e TIPO_PART. 
  • Realizar consultas em lotes, passando múltiplos lançamentos fiscais em cada consulta.  

Gostaríamos muito de saber o seu feedback. Deixe seu comentário abaixo caso tenha alguma dúvida ou sugestão para um próximo post. E não esqueça de seguir a tag SAP Tax Declaration Framework for Brazil aqui na SAP Community para ficar ligado nas últimas notícias sobre o Tax Declaration Framework (TDF). 

Até a próxima! 

Rafael Galuschka 

Equipe de desenvolvimento do SAP TDF

entry.title: Re: Consumo de FUE no SAP S/4HANA Cloud: uma visão geral em português
entry.content: Excellent.  Thanks

entry.title: SAP TDF - Performance Improvement for NF_IMPOSTO_RETIDO and PARTICIPANTE Views
entry.content:
Hi everybody, 

Recently, we delivered performance improvements for the views of CTR PARTICIPANTE and NF_IMPOSTO_RETIDO in notes 3462516 and 3520967 for SP18 and note 3538136 for SP19. The SAP TDF solution is already up to date to consume the enhancement delivered in the notes, but third-party implementations need to be adjusted to benefit from the performance changes. In this blog post we will describe the new parameters created and provide tips on how the improvements can be consumed. 

Optimization of the PARTICIPANTE view 

Some information on this view comes from invoices, so we have created the following parameters that can be used to decrease the volume of selected invoices: 

  • P_DT_INI: Start date of the invoice, compared to the Document date (DOCDAT) field. 
  • P_DT_FIN: End date of the invoice, compared to the Document date (DOCDAT) field. 
  • P_DT_E_S_INI: Start date of the invoice, compared to the TMF Reporting Date (ITMF_REPDAT) field. 
  • P_DT_E_S_FIN: Start date of the invoice, compared to the TMF Reporting Date (ITMF_REPDAT) field. 
  • P_DO_NOT_USE_ONE_TIME_PARTNER: In case ‘X’ is informed, participants derived in the invoice aren’t considered in the query. 

As the information coming from SAP ERP has no date field, the number of registers tends to grow. We recommend using the following PARTICIPANTE fields as filters: 

  • Participant Code (COD_PART) 
  • Partner Type (Vendor or Customer) (TIPO_PART) 

Using these filters significantly improves your queries involving the PARTICIPANTE view. 

Optimization of view NF_IMPOSTO_RETIDO 

The NF_IMPOSTO_RETIDO view consists of information coming from the PARTICIPANTE, taxes withheld from FI and invoices. The following parameters can be provided: 

  • P_MANDT: Client filter, relevant for all cases. 
  • P_EMPRESA: Company code, relevant for filtering both data coming from invoice and from taxes withheld from FI. 
  • P_DT_INI: Invoice start date, compared to the Document date (DOCDAT) field. 
  • P_DT_FIN: End date of the invoice, compared to the Document date (DOCDAT) field. 
  • P_DT_E_S_INI: Start date of the invoice, compared to the TMF Reporting Date (ITMF_REPDAT) field. 
  • P_DT_E_S_FIN: Start date of the invoice, compared to the TMF Reporting Date (ITMF_REPDAT) field. 
  • P_DO_NOT_USE_ONE_TIME_PARTNER: In case ‘X’ is informed, participants derived in the invoice are not considered in the query. 

Additionally, we recommend the following best practices when accessing the view: 

  • Use the PARTICIPANTE filters mentioned above: COD_PART and TIPO_PART. 
  • Perform batch queries by passing multiple tax postings in each query.  

We'd love to hear from you. Leave your comment below if you have any questions or suggestions for an upcoming post. And don't forget to follow the SAP Tax Declaration Framework for Brazil tag here in the SAP Community to get updated about the latest news about the Tax Declaration Framework (TDF). 

See you next time! 

Rafael Galuschka 

SAP TDF Development Team

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
"Excited to be a part of this vibrant SAP Community group! 🌟

Here's a little about me: I'm Madhusudan, currently diving deep into the world of SAP as I explore opportunities in procurement, supply chain, and logistics roles. With hands-on experience in roles that intersect technology and operations, I'm thrilled to connect with like-minded professionals and learn from this amazing community.

My journey with SAP has been fascinating, and I'd love to hear everyone's stories—especially the moment you first heard of or used SAP! Looking forward to engaging in lively discussions, sharing insights, and growing together. 🚀

Let's make this space even more dynamic—thank you for creating such a welcoming platform!"

entry.content:
Hi 

Please see this note 

3294673 - Warehouse Management Monitor: Correction of Issues with Hotspots

 

Regards

Rashid Khan

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation – SMTP Mail Destination using Microsoft 365 email provider
entry.content:
Hi @KarthikeyanMurthy 

Thank you very much for the blog, I was able to configure my M365 account thanks to the details you shared.

Perhaps this information will be useful to others, in my Microsoft account it looks slightly different (the step to disable MFA), I share the image so that you can orient yourself in case you have problems.

And, additionally, I share with you this code that helped me validate that the account and the M365 Application were working well:

 

const nodemailer = require('nodemailer');
const axios = require('axios');

// Configuración de OAuth2 Password
const tenantId = 'your tentant';
const clientId = 'your Client ID';
const clientSecret = 'your Secret ID';
const userEmail = 'your email';
const userPassword = 'your password'; 

// URL para obtener el token
const tokenUrl = `https://login.microsoftonline.com/${tenantId}/oauth2/v2.0/token`;

// Función para obtener el token de acceso
async function getAccessToken() {
    try {
        const response = await axios.post(tokenUrl, new URLSearchParams({
            client_id: clientId,
            client_secret: clientSecret,
            grant_type: 'password',
            username: userEmail,
            password: userPassword,
            scope: 'https://outlook.office.com/SMTP.send',
        }).toString(), {
            headers: {
                'Content-Type': 'application/x-www-form-urlencoded',
            },
        });
        return response.data.access_token;
    } catch (error) {
        console.error('Error obteniendo el token de acceso:', error.response?.data || error.message);
        throw error;
    }
}

// Función para enviar el correo
async function sendEmail() {
    try {
        const accessToken = await getAccessToken();

        // Configuración del transporte SMTP con OAuth2
        const transporter = nodemailer.createTransport({
            host: 'smtp.office365.com',
            port: 587,
            secure: false,
            auth: {
                type: 'OAuth2',
                user: userEmail,
                accessToken: accessToken,
            },
        });

        // Configuración del correo
        const mailOptions = {
            from: userEmail,
            to: 'destinatario@correo.com',
            subject: 'Prueba de correo con OAuth2Password',
            text: 'Este es un correo de prueba enviado desde Node.js utilizando OAuth2 Password y SMTP.',
        };

        // Envío del correo
        const info = await transporter.sendMail(mailOptions);
        console.log('Correo enviado:', info.response);
    } catch (error) {
        console.error('Error enviando el correo:', error.message);
    }
}

// Ejecutar la función
sendEmail();
 
I hope it is useful to all of you.

Best regards.

entry.title: Today is ROCKY DAY in the city of brotherly love! (What is your favorite Rocky movie??)
entry.content:
Hi NSQ peeps, are you celebrating?!? What is your favorite Rocky movie? I saw an interview earlier today with Sylvester Stallone-- he spoke about the simplicity of Rocky's message around hope. I liked the first Creed movie, and enjoy watching it over, and over again!

Rocky Day is a city holiday in Philadelphia that celebrates the release date of the original Rocky movie, which was on December 3, 1976. The first Rocky Day was celebrated in 2023, and the second annual Rocky Day will be on December 3, 2024.

Caio

entry.title: Flexible workflow: Recipients cannot be edited (The recipients are determined automatically.)
entry.content:
Flexible workflow: Recipients cannot be edited.

Previously I could edit the recipients of a workflow, but now I get the message “The recipients are determined automatically.”, and it does not allow to edit the recipients.
What can I do to make the recipients editable?

entry.title: Search function in Lumira Designer Release 2.4 SP0 Patch 6 (Version: 24.0.6)
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a search requirement for the lumira designer dashboard. The client wants to type material number/ material description and search the data and display on the dashbaord. The result should be displayed in the crosstab. I am using the following script written on the input field's change on event. The code is working fine, meaning it brings the data, but not all the matched data. if I increase the array size, its brings more data. For example if I write 50000 (var b = DS_1.getMembers("ZMATERIAL", 50000)), the code brings a few match records. 100000, brings more match records. Am I doing something wrong in writing the code? Please help me to fix the code.

var a = INPUTFIELD_1.getValue().toUpperCase(); // Convert input to upper case
var FilterList = "";
DS_1.setFilter("ZMATERIAL", a);

var b = DS_1.getMembers("ZMATERIAL", 50000);

APPLICATION.alert(a);


// If input is blank, set a to a value that ensures DS_2 is used
if (a.trim() === "") {
a = "DS2"; // Use a placeholder value to ensure it won't match any valid entries
}

b.forEach(function(element, index) {
// var elementTextUpper = element.text.toUpperCase(); // Convert element text to upper case
// var elementKeyUpper = element.externalKey.toUpperCase(); // Convert element key to upper case

// Check if input matches either the text or the key
if (Convert.indexOf(element.text, a) != -1 || Convert.indexOf(element.internalKey, a) != -1) {
FilterList = FilterList + element.externalKey + ";";
}
});

DS_1.setFilterExt("ZMATERIAL", FilterList);

// Logic to hide or show CROSSTAB_1 based on filters
var filters = DS_1.getFilterExt("ZMATERIAL"); // Changed to "ZMATERIAL"
if (filters.length > 0) {
CROSSTAB_1.setVisible(true);
CROSSTAB_1.setDataSource(DS_1);
IMAGE_1.setVisible(true);
} else {
CROSSTAB_1.setDataSource(DS_2);
CROSSTAB_1.setVisible(false); // Ensure crosstab is hidden when using DS_2
}

// Explicitly hide the crosstab if the input is blank
if (a === "DS2") {
IMAGE_1.setVisible(false);
CROSSTAB_1.setVisible(false);

}

entry.title: Re: Hana Cloud Dashboard "Uh oh. Something went amiss."
entry.content: Thanks it worked

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
@Madhusudan_7774 it is great to meet you. I live in the US outside of Philadelphia and am based in the SAP Newtown Square office. I heard of SAP many years ago when I was a supply chain manager. Since then, I have spent a number of years with SAP mostly as an end-user of SAP software to do my job. 

The SAP Community is the happening spot to meet SAPers from all over the world! Wishing you the best of luck!

entry.title: Re: Focused Build Customer Extension Field Changes to Metadata Configuration and Process Type View,
entry.content:
Hello @cgovind , and thank you very much for the extensive contributions!

Can you give any tips on what post activities needs to be done after importing the configuration into the productive SolMan? We have successfully configured custom fields (for Defect S1DM, everything works in DEV), but after transport, the fields are not visible in production SolMan.

We have already carried out the following refreshes:

  • SMICM: Goto -> HTTP plugin -> Server cache -> Invalidate Locally and Globally
  • Transaction /IWFND/CACHE_CLEANUP
  • Transaction /IWBEP/CACHE_CLEANUP
  • Reports:
    • /UI2/CHIP_SYNCHRONIZE_CACHE
    • /UI2/DELETE_CACHE_AFTER_IMP
    • /UI2/DELETE_CACHE
    • /UI5/APP_INDEX_CALCULATE
    • /UI2/INVALIDATE_GLOBAL_CACHES
  • + Browser Cache refresh

In transaction /IWFND/GW_CLIENT -> /sap/opu/odata/salm/CRM_GENERIC_SRV/$metadata

The fields are available but it’s not displayed in MyDefect application (only area “Custom Field” is being displayed).

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: How to Use cds.test() for Testing On-Premise Connecti...
entry.content: I would create a separate testing profile that has the necessary connection information and then use cds.test with a profile reference. Any reason that would not work for you?

entry.title: Re: changeset processing in sap cap
entry.content:
I had a similar problem and had to code it with a custom action handler. None of the standard examples I could find would work. so here is what I did....

sap.ui.define([
    "sap/m/MessageBox"
], function (MessageBox) {
    'use strict';

    return {
        onActionKickoff: async function (oBindingContext, aSelectedContexts) {

  // Specify the action name
            const sActionName = "GuiService.kickOff";
            const sNavSemanticObject = "AoObj";
            const sNavAction = "view";


            // Process the contexts - if an array of contexts is passed, only the first one is processed
            // The context does not respond with a valid object if an array is passed in
            if (Array.isArray(aSelectedContexts)) {
                aSelectedContexts = aSelectedContexts[0];
            }


            // Specify the parameters for the action
            const mParameters = {
                contexts: aSelectedContexts,
                model: aSelectedContexts.model,
                label: 'Confirm',
                invocationGrouping: true
            };

            // Invoke the backend action
            const actionResult = await this.editFlow.invokeAction(sActionName, mParameters); //SAP Fiori elements EditFlow API
 
...
}}

entry.title: Scenario 2: Workflow to Verify General Journal Entries per Submitter
entry.content:
Configuring workflows for the first time in SAP S/4HANA Cloud may seem complicated, but it is not.

In this blog, I will show scenario 2 of configuring the workflow to assign the Approver based on the user who creates the JE (Submitter) and Company Code.

The following example was executed in an SAP Internal System. The activation of the Workflow to Verify General Journal Entries is based on the documentation provided by the SAP Help Portal - Example of Adding Custom Fields Link

Use case Example:

Let's assume the requirement is the following:

The Cloud Group has 2 Company Codes (1010 and 1730), 2 General Ledger Accountants (Edna and Josie), and 2 General Ledger Managers (Enrico and Paul).

All the general journal entries submitted by Josie in the company code Paul must be approved at 1730.

Enrico must approve the general journal entries submitted by Edna in company code 1730. However, the postings created in company code 1010 do not need approval. 

Configuration Process Flow:

The configuration consists of five parts:

    1. Create the ZSUBMITBY Field.
    2. Create Custom Functions.
    3. Define the Approvers groups in Teams and Responsibilities.
    4. Activate the workflow for the Approvers and Submitters.
    5. Activate the workflow for the Non-Approval process.

Step 1: Create a custom field - ZSUBMITBY.

1.1 Create the new custom field.

Open the configuration step 102159 - Define Custom Responsibility Definition in your configuration environment.

  • Click the ‘New Entries’ button to create a new entry
  • Fill in the following fields:
    • Resp. Def. Name: Z_SUBMITBY
    • CDS View: I_BUSINESSUSERBASIC
    • CDS Field: USERID
    • Resp. Def. Name: Z_SubmitBy
    • Resp. Def. Desc: User ID
    • Press Enter
    • Click on the Save button

1.2 Assign Custom Responsibility Definition to CDS View

Open the configuration step 102163 - Assign Custom Responsibility Definition to CDS View in your configuration environment.

  • Click the ‘New Entries’ button to create a new entry
  • Fill in the following fields:
    • Customer Responsibility Definition: Z_SUBMITBY
    • CDS VIEW name: C_GLJRNLENTRYTOBEVERIFIED
    • CDS field name: PARKEDBYUSER
    • Press Enter
  • Click on the Save button

1.3 Assign Custom Responsibility Definitions to Standard Team Category

Open the configuration step 102160 - Assign Custom Responsibility Definitions to Standard Team Category in your configuration environment.

  • Click the ‘New Entries’ button to create a new entry
  • Fill in the following fields:
    • Category: FGJEV (G/L Journal Entry Verification)
    • Customer Responsibility Definition: Z_SUBMITBY
    • Press Enter to populate the Responsibility Definition Description field.
  • Click on the Save button

Step 2: Create Custom Functions

2.1 Define Functions

Open the configuration step 101065 - Define Functions in your configuration environment.

  • Create the functions to define the tasks that need to be executed for the team members.

2.2 Define Function Profiles.

Open the configuration step 101065 - Define Functions Profiles in your configuration environment.

  • Map the function to the corresponding Function Profile, linked to the Type field in the Teams and Responsibilities app.

Step 3: Define the Approvers groups in Teams and Responsibilities.

3.1 Teams and Responsibilities App.

  • Create a Team for every group of approvers.
  • Teams and responsibilities app is used to define who is responsible for workflows and situation handling.
  • The responsibility Definitions are different for each Type of Team.
  • The Owner is responsible for maintaining the team header, responsibility definitions, and team members and their functions.
  • The Team Members are the users who will receive the corresponding workflow (approvers).
  • The Function is a task that a team member can perform as a part of the business process.
  • Example of the Team APPROVER01

3.1 Teams and Responsibilities App.

  • Example of the Team APPROVER03

Step 4: Activate the workflow for the Approvers and Submitters.

4.1 Manage Workflow for Journal Entry Verification in General Ledger App

  • Select and copy the standard “General Journal Entry Verification” workflow.

  • You only need one workflow for all the Teams using the same function in the Teams and Responsibilities app.
  • In the Start Conditions section, DO NOT add all the company codes to the same field. Use the function “or” to get the correct functionality.

  • In the “Verify” step, assign the function in the Assignment field by selecting Assignment By Role.
  • Save the Changes
  • Activate your workflow

Step 5: Activate the workflow for the Non-Approval process.

5.1 Manage Workflow for Journal Entry Verification in General Ledger App

  • The following workflow is optional.
  • It is created when you do not need approvers for specific conditions.  For example, a specific Company Code

Remember to look at Scenario 1: Workflow to Verify General Journal Entries per Level by Amount Blog for more details about the Roles of the Requester and Processor.

I hope this information is helpful. If it is, please press like to keep publishing more blogs.

entry.title: Re: DRFOUT Error
entry.content: This issue has been resolved , it is basically due to miss in the configuration which is related to Maintain control parameters for Hierarchies.

entry.title: Re: No user token (JWT) has been provided in cds.connect....
entry.content: In my experience, the destination test in the Cockpit does not work for this type of Authentication method. The method assumes that as part of the request you are providing a JWT token and the Cockpit does not automatically create a valid JWT for your uaa service for you and pass it in as part of the test. You can probably write yourself a testing tool to check the destination outside of the Cockpit, but that will require some more fancy footwork.

entry.title: Multiple input parameter is not working for DOES NOT CONTAINS operator in Define conditions
entry.content:
In define conditions multiple input parameters are working for Contains Operators but not working for DOES NOT CONTAINS operator. 

In Consumption CDS view we have defined the valuehelp

@ui.lineItem: [{ position : 2 , label: 'Notification'},
{type: #FOR_INTENT_BASED_NAVIGATION, semanticObjectAction: 'change'}]
@Consumption.semanticObject:'PRID_ISSUE'
@EndUserText.label: 'Notification'
@ui.selectionField:[{position:2}]
@ui.identification: [{position: 2, importance: #HIGH }]
@Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{entity:{element:'qmnum',
name:'Z_notification_VH'} }]
@search.defaultSearchElement: true
@search.fuzzinessThreshold: 0.7
@search.ranking : #HIGH
key qmnum

 

In ODATA we have added the logic to filter dynamically. 

 lo_facade      ?= me->/iwbep/if_mgw_conv_srv_runtime~get_dp_facade( ).

In IT_FILTER_SELECT_OPTIONS values are not storing in internal table when we search for multiple values.

Can anyone help me to solve the issue as soon as possible.

Thanks and Regards,

Sushma

entry.title: Re: '/SCWM/DLV_ITEM_UPDATE_PAR' CANCEL OUTBOUND DN
entry.content: Hi, how is it solved?

entry.title: HANA DB Replication
entry.content:
Hi,

While trying to re-sync the On-prem HANA DB DR replication, the status shown in Hana studio in percentage is getting reverse, like as it reach to 10%, then suddenly it drops back to 8%, have tried setting replication both ways sync and async but still same result.

What might be the reason, please advise. If anybody have experience this.

entry.title: Re: Variant configuration status through tables or fm
entry.content:
Though the question is answered. but just want to update that each checkbox is internally calling a standalone report available to be called separately. here are all those 10 reports-

Report NoReport IDReport
1RCUCHECK_VBAP_IBASE1. VBAP entries without IBASE / OLDDATABASE entries (RCUCHECK_VBAP_IBASE
2RCUCHECK_VBBE_VBAP2. VBBE entries without VBAP entries (RCUCHECK_VBBE_VBAP
3RCUCHECK_MARA_IBASE3. MARA entries without IBASE / OLDDATABASE entries (RCUCHECK_MARA_IBASE
4RCUCHECK_MARC_IBASE4. MARC entries without IBASE / OLDDATABASE entries (RCUCHECK_MARC_IBASE
5RCUCHECK_STPO_CUOB5. STPO-knobj entries without CUOB-knobj entries (RCUCHECK_STPO_CUOB
6RCUCHECK_CUOB_CUKB6. CUOB entries without CUKB entries (RCUCHECK_CUOB_CUKB
7RCUCHECK_KDST_STKO7. KDST entries without STKO entries (RCUCHECK_KDST_STKO
8RCUCHECK_DOUBLE_INOB8. Repeated INOB entries (RCUCHECK_DOUBLE_INOB
9RCUCHECK_INOB_EQUI9. Incorrect INOB-OBTAB entries with owner EQUI (RCUCHECK_INOB_EQUI
10RCUCHECK_DUPLICATE_IBST10. Repeated IBST entries (RCUCHECK_DUPLICATE_IBST

entry.title: Re: Variant configuration status through tables or fm
entry.content: #VCReport1865

entry.title: Re: Deleting single component of a subcontracting PO
entry.content:
Hi Naveena,

I'm facing the same issue. Have you found a solution or a workaround?

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Exploring the Database Objects App and SQL Console in SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content:
I am thrilled to introduce a new tutorial in the Get Started with the SAP HANA Database Explorer group, Browse and Explore Catalog Objects with the Database Objects App. The Database Objects app and SQL Console are two powerful tools that allow users to manage and explore catalog objects efficiently directly from SAP HANA Cloud Central. We are continuing to include functionality from the SAP HANA database explorer into SAP HANA Cloud Central providing one central tool for administration and monitoring. 

Introduction to the Database Objects App and SQL Console 

The Database Objects app is an intuitive catalog browser interface within SAP HANA Cloud Central (HCC) that enables users to search, filter, and manage a wide range of database objects. The SQL Console provides a development environment for executing SQL queries, with helpful features like error indicators, result set formatting options, and a statement library. These new features and capabilities are available exclusively in the multi-environment edition while the Cloud Foundry edition of SAP HANA Cloud Central is now deprecated. 

Key Features and Updates 

Here is a breakdown of what you can now do with these tools.

Database Objects App:

  • Sort, Group, and Show/Hide Columns: Tailor your view for better organization and have faster access to key information. 
  • Expanded Object Access: View tasks, graph workspaces, HDI containers, JSON collections, remote sources, and synonyms within SAP HANA database instances. 
  • Customization Options and Improved Convenience: Select which database objects to display, reorder them with drag-and-drop, and save frequently used items to the Favorites tab for quick access. While the Recent tab provides easy access to recently viewed objects. 
  • SQL Generation: From any object’s details page, you can generate SQL statements specific to that schema or object. 

SQL Console:

  • Editor and Library Mode: Easily toggle between editing and viewing saved statements, enabling a streamlined workflow.
  • Real-Time Parsing: Catch errors and warnings on the go with real-time SQL parsing, which highlights issues directly in the SQL editor.
  • Duplicate Tabs: Open multiple tabs within an instance for multitasking and comparing SQL statements. 
  • Advanced Export Options: Customize SQL exports by replacing newlines with spaces or line feed strings for smoother data handling. 

To view all the latest new features, navigate to: What's New in SAP HANA Cloud

Tutorial Overview

The new tutorial provides a hands-on approach to mastering these features related to Database Objects App. Here’s what you’ll learn: 

  • Filtering for Tables and Schemas: Navigate the Database Objects app and use filters to find specific tables or schemas within an instance. 
  • Exploring Objects and Metadata: Inspect metadata, such as ownership and creation times, for schemas and objects. You can explore various tabs like Tables for column details and runtime information.
  • SQL Generation: Generate SQL statements for specific database objects, by simply clicking a button. 
  • Favorites and Recent Tabs: Organize frequently accessed database objects for quicker navigation in future sessions. 

Additionally, there also exists a step-by-step tutorial on the features related to the SQL Console (Query Databases Using the SQL Console in SAP HANA Cloud Central). Here is what you’ll learn: 

  • Opening SQL Consoles: Learn how to quickly open multiple SQL consoles from within HCC, specify credentials, and set the current schema.  
  • Executing SQL Queries: Execute SQL queries, examine statement help, and view query results, messages, and history tabs within the SQL console. 
  • Settings, Viewer and other tools: Navigate the SQL Console settings feature to customize SQL formatting. Utilize the viewer to expand and examine results. Import and export SQL code by simply clicking a button.

Each of these tutorials walks you through using SAP HANA Cloud Central’s Database Objects App and SQL Console, allowing you to streamline and improve your database management experience. 

Why Try the Database Objects App and SQL Console? 

With these new features, SAP HANA Cloud’s Database Objects app and SQL Console are more convenient and user-friendly than ever. Users can now manage all their tasks from HCC, reducing the need to switch between tools and improving efficiency. I encourage you to try out the tutorials and explore how these features can make a difference in your workflow. Your feedback is invaluable, let us know how these new tools help you in managing and interacting with your database objects. Leave any comments or feedback below or in the tutorial by selecting the feedback option.

entry.title: Re: Why is Hungarian Notation Harmful?
entry.content: After more than 25 years of ABAP I strongly resisted the idea of dropping the prefixes. Nonetheless I tried it because some other senior developers said, that they like the cleaner code without the prefixes. So I gave it a try and to my surprise - I don't like prefixes any more! It's 100% as you state: Naming variables takes a bit more time and definately more thought, but the code improves by much more meaningful variable names.
